title: Rendering foundation — style system, scroll region helpers, fixed panel compositor
purpose: Every TUI subsystem (markdown rendering, tool output, input area, agent status) needs two things: a style abstraction that adapts to the terminal's capabilities, and a rendering surface. This brief builds both — the TerminalCaps-aware style layer and the two rendering surfaces (scroll region append helpers, fixed panel compositor). After this brief lands, other briefs can write styled content to the scroll region and render components in the fixed panel.
task: Implement the style system in render/style.rs: colour mapping (true colour → 256-colour fallback), attribute helpers (italic → underline fallback), OSC 8 hyperlink helper (→ bracketed text fallback), synchronized rendering wrappers (→ cursor hide/show fallback). Implement scroll region write helpers in render/scroll_region.rs: position cursor within DECSTBM bounds, append styled text, handle newlines that push content up within the scroll region. Implement the fixed panel compositor in render/fixed_panel.rs: component registry tracking which components are active (agent lines, streaming indicator, autocomplete popup, input area, status bar), total height calculation, full-panel redraw within synchronized rendering, status bar renderer showing model name, session info, token usage, and key hints. Ensure fixed panel redraws never affect scroll region content.

requirements.title: Colour mapping with true colour fallback
requirements.spec: WHEN the style system renders a colour, THE SYSTEM SHALL use 24-bit RGB when TerminalCaps.true_colour is true AND SHALL fall back to the nearest 256-colour palette entry otherwise.
acceptance: colour_for(rgb, caps) returns an RGB escape when true_colour is true
colour_for(rgb, caps) returns the nearest 256-colour index when true_colour is false
256-colour mapping uses Euclidean distance in RGB space across the standard 256-colour palette
Test: specific RGB value maps to expected 256-colour index

requirements.title: Attribute helpers with capability fallbacks
requirements.spec: The style system SHALL provide attribute helpers that fall back gracefully when the terminal lacks support: italic → underline, Kitty keyboard Shift+Enter → Alt+Enter escape sequence.
acceptance: italic(caps) returns italic SGR when italic_support is true, underline SGR otherwise
newline_key_hint(caps) returns 'Shift+Enter' when kitty_keyboard is true, 'Alt+Enter' otherwise
Test: italic fallback produces underline SGR when italic_support is false

requirements.title: OSC 8 hyperlink helper
requirements.spec: WHEN the style system renders a hyperlink, THE SYSTEM SHALL emit an OSC 8 escape sequence when TerminalCaps.osc_hyperlinks is true AND SHALL fall back to 'text (url)' bracketed format otherwise.
acceptance: hyperlink(text, url, caps) returns OSC 8 sequence when osc_hyperlinks is true
hyperlink(text, url, caps) returns 'text (url)' string when osc_hyperlinks is false
OSC 8 format: ESC ] 8 ; ; url ST text ESC ] 8 ; ; ST
Test: hyperlink with osc_hyperlinks=false produces bracketed format

requirements.title: Synchronized rendering wrapper
requirements.spec: WHEN the fixed panel redraws, THE SYSTEM SHALL wrap the redraw in synchronized rendering markers (DCS 2026) when TerminalCaps.synchronized_rendering is true AND SHALL hide/show the cursor during redraws otherwise.
acceptance: sync_render(caps, || { ... }) wraps the closure in DCS 2026h / DCS 2026l when synchronized_rendering is true
sync_render(caps, || { ... }) wraps the closure in cursor hide / cursor show when synchronized_rendering is false
Test: sync_render with synchronized_rendering=false produces cursor hide/show sequences

requirements.title: Scroll region write helpers
requirements.spec: The scroll region module SHALL provide helpers to append styled content at the cursor position within the DECSTBM scroll region bounds.
acceptance: write_to_scroll(content, writer) writes content at the current cursor position within the scroll region
Newlines within content push earlier lines up within the scroll region (native terminal scrollback behaviour)
Content written to the scroll region is never overwritten by subsequent calls
write_separator(label, width, writer) writes a full-width separator line
Test: write_to_scroll produces output containing the given content string

requirements.title: Fixed panel compositor
requirements.spec: The fixed panel compositor SHALL track active components, calculate total panel height, and perform full-panel redraws within synchronized rendering that do not affect scroll region content.
acceptance: FixedPanel struct tracks which components are active: agent_lines (0-5), streaming_indicator (0-1), autocomplete_popup (0-8), input_area (1-N), status_bar (1)
total_height() returns the sum of active component heights
redraw(writer, caps) clears and redraws all active components within synchronized rendering, cursor-addressed to the fixed panel rows only
Redraw writes to rows (terminal_height - total_height) through (terminal_height - 1), never touching scroll region rows
When total_height changes, the compositor calls reissue_scroll_region() from NT-001
Test: total_height returns correct sum for a given set of active components
Test: redraw output does not contain cursor movements into scroll region rows

requirements.title: Status bar
requirements.spec: The status bar component SHALL render model name, session info, token usage, and key hints in a single row at the bottom of the fixed panel.
acceptance: StatusBar struct holds: model_name, session_name, input_tokens, output_tokens, key_hints
render(row, width, writer, caps) writes a styled single-line status bar at the specified row
Model name and session info left-aligned, token usage and key hints right-aligned
Key hints show the newline key appropriate to capabilities (Shift+Enter or Alt+Enter per R2)
Test: render produces output containing the model name and token counts

requirements.title: Streaming indicator component
requirements.spec: The streaming indicator component SHALL show generating status with elapsed time while the model is producing output AND SHALL show usage summary after completion.
acceptance: StreamingIndicator enum: Idle (not rendered, 0 rows), Generating { elapsed: Duration } (1 row), Complete { usage_summary: String } (1 row)
Generating renders as '● generating... {elapsed}s' with a distinct colour
Complete renders the usage summary string matching the existing format_usage_summary style
Idle contributes 0 rows to the fixed panel height
Test: Generating state renders with elapsed time

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement markdown parsing or syntax highlighting — that is NT-003
SHALL NOT implement input editing or key handling — that is NT-004
SHALL NOT implement tool-specific rendering — that is NT-006/NT-007
SHALL NOT implement the event loop or ProviderEvent handling — that is NT-011
SHALL NOT wire live data into the status bar or streaming indicator — NT-011 wires the data; this brief builds the rendering frame
SHALL NOT add todo!() or unimplemented!() macros anywhere
verification: Style colour mapping produces correct fallback for 256-colour terminals — verified by test
Italic fallback produces underline — verified by test
OSC 8 fallback produces bracketed text — verified by test
Fixed panel compositor height calculation correct — verified by test
Scroll region write helpers produce output — verified by test
